title: alopecurus pratensis subsp. alpestris
intro: Alopecurus pratensis subsp. alpestris, also known as the Alpine Meadow Foxtail or Alpine Meadow-grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is native to mountain meadows and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against oxidative stress, potentially offering health benefits such as improved cardiovascular health and reduced risk of chronic diseases. Herbal preparations of Alopecurus pratensis subsp. alpestris may include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
